transcript:
Right now you're watching 550 broken rings drift into a slow-breathing halo — each one an incomplete circle, a little mouth of missing arc, searching the dark for its match.

Here's the thing that first frame hides: every shape is drawn with a gap in it. Look in the code at "renderFragment" — the arc is drawn from "arcStart" to "arcEnd," deliberately skipping a piece called "absenceArc." That's not decoration, that's the whole premise — each fragment is missing a specific, sized bite of itself. Then in "calculateRecognition," two fragments only warm up when they're "mutualComplement" — when one's hunger direction is literally the other's opposite. They don't fuse. They just get brighter, more saturated, more present — you can see it as "warmth" driving color and alpha directly.

So the ring you're watching resolve isn't unity. It's hundreds of specific holes finding the one shape that fits them — and glowing only as long as that fit holds.
